title: Balanced bracket checker
prompt: Read a single line of input from standard input and decide whether the bracket characters ( ) [ ] { } are balanced. A string is balanced when every opener has a later matching closer of the same kind, every closer matches the most recent unmatched opener, and the openers/closers nest without crossing. All non-bracket characters are ignored. The empty string is balanced. Write yes followed by a newline if the input is balanced, otherwise write no followed by a newline. Exit with status 0. The input is at most 1000 characters and uses only printable ASCII.

input_schema.stdin: <one line of printable ASCII, up to 1000 characters, terminated by \n>

notes_input_convention: Zero reads the input from argv[1] because Zero 0.1.2 has no exposed stdin capability. All other languages read one line from stdin (terminator stripped). The harness adapts per language.
